WITH RECURSIVE 
item_list AS (
    SELECT cost, available_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY cost) as idx
    FROM items
    WHERE cost > 0
),
knapsack(idx, remaining_budget, total_qty) AS (
    SELECT 0, 100.0, 0
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        i.idx,
        k.remaining_budget - LEAST(FLOOR(k.remaining_budget / i.cost), i.available_quantity) * i.cost,
        k.total_qty + LEAST(FLOOR(k.remaining_budget / i.cost), i.available_quantity)
    FROM knapsack k
    JOIN item_list i ON i.idx = k.idx + 1
    WHERE k.idx < (SELECT MAX(idx) FROM item_list)
)
SELECT MAX(total_qty) as total_quantity FROM knapsack

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE(
            SUBSTR(CAST(fe.start_date_key AS TEXT), 1, 4) || '-' || 
            SUBSTR(CAST(fe.start_date_key AS TEXT), 5, 2) || '-' || 
            SUBSTR(CAST(fe.start_date_key AS TEXT), 7, 2)
        ) AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN fe.end_date_key IS NOT NULL THEN 
                DATE(
                    SUBSTR(CAST(fe.end_date_key AS TEXT), 1, 4) || '-' || 
                    SUBSTR(CAST(fe.end_date_key AS TEXT), 5, 2) || '-' || 
                    SUBSTR(CAST(fe.end_date_key AS TEXT), 7, 2)
                )
        END AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code,
        fe.card_key 
    FROM fact_collection AS fe 
    INNER JOIN dim_set AS dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT DATE('2023-01-01') AS month_start, DATE('2023-01-31') AS month_end
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(month_start, '+1 month'), DATE(month_end, '+1 month') 
    FROM months 
    WHERE month_start < '2024-12-01'
)
SELECT 
    SUBSTR(CAST(m.month_start AS TEXT), 1, 7) AS month,
    ca.set_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ca.card_key) AS existing_cards
FROM months m
LEFT JOIN card_activity AS ca 
    ON ca.start_date <= m.month_end 
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date > m.month_start)
GROUP BY m.month_start, ca.set_name
ORDER BY m.month_start, ca.set_name
LIMIT 12

WITH mission_spans AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id, 
        mission_start, 
        mission_end,
        LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS prev_end
    FROM superhero_missions
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN prev_end IS NULL OR mission_start != prev_end THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS grp
    FROM mission_spans
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    MIN(mission_start) AS span_start,
    MAX(mission_end) AS span_end
FROM grouped
GROUP BY hero_id, grp
ORDER BY hero_id, span_start

WITH RECURSIVE
cols AS (
  SELECT name, cid, 
         lead(cid) OVER (ORDER BY cid) as next_cid,
         lag(cid) OVER (ORDER BY cid) as prev_cid
  FROM pragma_table_info('superhero')
  WHERE name LIKE '%_id'
  ORDER BY cid
),
col_array AS (
  SELECT json_group_array(json_object('name', name, 'cid', cid)) as arr
  FROM cols
),
numbered AS (
  SELECT 
    json_extract(value, '$.name') as col_name,
    json_extract(value, '$.cid') as col_id,
    row_number() OVER (ORDER BY json_extract(value, '$.cid')) as n
  FROM col_array, json_each(arr)
),
build_json(hero_id, json_so_far, n) AS (
  SELECT 
    s.id,
    json_object(n.col_name, 
      CASE n.col_name
        WHEN 'gender_id' THEN s.gender_id
        WHEN 'eye_colour_id' THEN s.eye_colour_id
        WHEN 'hair_colour_id' THEN s.hair_colour_id
        WHEN 'skin_colour_id' THEN s.skin_colour_id
        WHEN 'race_id' THEN s.race_id
        WHEN 'publisher_id' THEN s.publisher_id
        WHEN 'alignment_id' THEN s.alignment_id
      END
    ),
    1
  FROM superhero s
  CROSS JOIN (SELECT * FROM numbered WHERE n = 1) n
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    b.hero_id,
    json_patch(b.json_so_far, json_object(n.col_name,
      CASE n.col_name
        WHEN 'gender_id' THEN s.gender_id
        WHEN 'eye_colour_id' THEN s.eye_colour_id
        WHEN 'hair_colour_id' THEN s.hair_colour_id
        WHEN 'skin_colour_id' THEN s.skin_colour_id
        WHEN 'race_id' THEN s.race_id
        WHEN 'publisher_id' THEN s.publisher_id
        WHEN 'alignment_id' THEN s.alignment_id
      END
    )),
    b.n + 1
  FROM build_json b
  JOIN numbered n ON n.n = b.n + 1
  JOIN superhero s ON s.id = b.hero_id
  WHERE b.n < (SELECT max(n) FROM numbered)
)
SELECT json_so_far as id_columns_json
FROM build_json
WHERE n = (SELECT max(n) FROM numbered)

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Recursive CTE to fully explode all crafting requirements
-- At each level, we replace items with their components
craft_tree AS (
    -- Base case: start with each item's direct requirements
    SELECT 
        s.item_id AS root_item,
        COALESCE(s.resource_id, CAST(s.uses_item_id AS INTEGER)) AS component_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN 'resource'
            ELSE 'item'
        END AS component_type,
        s.quantity,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN s.resource_price
            ELSE s.uses_item_price
        END AS unit_price,
        s.quantity * CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN s.resource_price
            ELSE s.uses_item_price
        END AS extended_price,
        1 AS level
    FROM store s
    WHERE (s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan') 
       OR (s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL AND s.uses_item_id != 'nan')
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: expand item components into their requirements
    SELECT 
        ct.root_item,
        COALESCE(s.resource_id, CAST(s.uses_item_id AS INTEGER)),
        CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN 'resource'
            ELSE 'item'
        END,
        ct.quantity * s.quantity,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN s.resource_price
            ELSE s.uses_item_price
        END,
        ct.quantity * s.quantity * CASE 
            WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan' THEN s.resource_price
            ELSE s.uses_item_price
        END,
        ct.level + 1
    FROM craft_tree ct
    JOIN store s ON ct.component_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ct.component_type = 'item'
      AND ct.level < 10
      AND ((s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND s.resource_id != 'nan') 
           OR (s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL AND s.uses_item_id != 'nan'))
),

-- Calculate partial craft price (resources only, level 1)
partial_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        SUM(CASE 
            WHEN resource_id IS NOT NULL AND resource_id != 'nan' 
            THEN resource_price * quantity 
            ELSE 0 
        END) AS partial_craft_price
    FROM store
    GROUP BY item_id
),

-- Calculate full craft price (all leaf resources after full expansion)
-- We need only the leaf nodes (resources at the deepest level)
full_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        root_item AS item_id,
        SUM(extended_price) AS full_craft_price
    FROM craft_tree
    WHERE component_type = 'resource'  -- only count final resources
       OR component_id IS NULL  -- or if no expansion possible
    GROUP BY root_item
)

SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    MAX(s.item_price) AS retail_price,
    MAX(pc.partial_craft_price) AS partial_craft_price,
    COALESCE(MAX(fc.full_craft_price), 0) AS full_craft_price
FROM store s
LEFT JOIN partial_craft pc ON s.item_id = pc.item_id
LEFT JOIN full_craft fc ON s.item_id = fc.item_id
GROUP BY s.item_id
ORDER BY s.item_id

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Convert to MST (UTC-7, or UTC-6 during daylight saving - using -7 here)
-- Adjust event_date to MST timezone first, then get distinct days
mst_events AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        DATE(event_date, '-7 hours') AS event_day
    FROM event 
    WHERE event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
-- Add row numbers to help identify streaks
numbered AS (
    SELECT 
        event_day,
        JULIANDAY(event_day) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day) AS grp
    FROM mst_events
),
-- Group by streak identifier to find streaks
streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length,
        MIN(event_day) AS streak_start,
        MAX(event_day) AS streak_end
    FROM numbered
    GROUP BY grp
)
-- Get the most recent streak (highest end date)
SELECT 
    streak_length AS streak_count,
    'April Meeting' AS event_name,
    'MST' AS timezone,
    streak_start AS start_date,
    streak_end AS end_date
FROM streaks
ORDER BY streak_end DESC
LIMIT 1

SELECT a.id, a.date, a.got, a.gpt

INNER JOIN (
    SELECT l.id, MIN(l.date) AS min_date

    INNER JOIN (

        FROM laboratory l1
        INNER JOIN (
            SELECT id, MAX(date) AS max_date
            FROM laboratory
            GROUP BY id
        ) l2 ON l1.id = l2.id AND l1.date = l2.max_date
    ) max_vals ON l.id = max_vals.id AND l.got = max_vals.got AND l.gpt = max_vals.gpt

) b ON a.id = b.id AND a.date = b.min_date
ORDER BY a.id, a.date

WITH RECURSIVE ManagerChain AS (
    -- Base case: start with each employee and their direct boss
    SELECT 
        s.id AS employeeId,
        s.salary AS employeeSalary,
        s.bossid,
        boss.salary AS bossSalary,
        1 AS hierarchical_level
    FROM staff s
    LEFT JOIN staff boss ON s.bossid = boss.id
    WHERE s.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: climb up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        mc.employeeId,
        mc.employeeSalary,
        s.bossid,
        s.salary AS bossSalary,
        mc.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM ManagerChain mc
    INNER JOIN staff s ON mc.bossid = s.id
    WHERE s.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
QualifiedIndirectManagers AS (
    -- Find indirect managers (level > 1) who earn at least twice as much
    SELECT 
        employeeId,
        employeeSalary,
        hierarchical_level,
        bossid AS bossId,
        bossSalary,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY employeeId 
            ORDER BY hierarchical_level ASC
        ) AS rn
    FROM ManagerChain
    WHERE bossSalary >= 2 * employeeSalary
      AND hierarchical_level > 1  -- Must be an indirect manager
)
SELECT 
    employeeId,
    employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    bossId,
    bossSalary
FROM QualifiedIndirectManagers
WHERE rn = 1  -- First (lowest-ranked/closet) qualifying indirect manager
ORDER BY employeeId

WITH hero_attribute AS (
    SELECT column1 as hero_id, column2 as attribute_type, column3 as attribute_value 
    FROM (VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70))
),
params AS (
    SELECT 2 as n, 2 as k, 2 as kp
),
group_definitions AS (
    SELECT 1 as group_num, 1 as start_row, (SELECT n + kp FROM params) as end_row
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 2, 1, (SELECT n + k + kp FROM params)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 3, (SELECT n + 1 FROM params), (SELECT n + k + kp FROM params)
)
SELECT SUM(ha.attribute_value) as sum
FROM group_definitions gd
JOIN hero_attribute ha ON ha.hero_id >= gd.start_row AND ha.hero_id <= gd.end_row
GROUP BY gd.group_num
ORDER BY gd.group_num

WITH skill_data AS (

        'crossing' AS skill_name,
        pa.crossing AS skill_value

CREATE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON Team_Attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
BEGIN 
    -- Update the previous "current" record for this team to close it out
    -- Set its eff_to to one day before the new record's date
    UPDATE Team_Attributes 
    SET eff_to = date(NEW.date, '-1 day')
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id 
      AND id != NEW.id
      AND date < NEW.date
      AND eff_to = '5999-12-31';
    
    -- Calculate the new record's eff_to based on any future record for the same team
    -- If no future record exists, set to '5999-12-31'
    UPDATE Team_Attributes 
    SET eff_to = COALESCE(
        (SELECT date(MIN(date), '-1 day') 
         FROM Team_Attributes 
         WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id 
           AND date > NEW.date), 
        '5999-12-31'
    )
    WHERE id = NEW.id;
END

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Calculate depth for each node
d AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, color, LENGTH(id) - 1 as depth
    FROM superhero2
),
max_d AS (
    SELECT MAX(depth) as md FROM d
),
-- Recursive CTE that propagates colors upward
-- We track: current node, its computed color, and the level we're processing
prop(node_id, node_color, node_depth, lvl) AS (
    -- Start with all leaves (nodes at max depth)
    SELECT id, color, depth, 0
    FROM d
    WHERE depth = (SELECT md FROM max_d)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- For each iteration, find parents of nodes at current frontier
    -- and compute their colors
    SELECT 
        par.id,
        CASE
            -- Count how many children this parent has in our frontier
            -- and determine their colors
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(*) FROM prop 
                WHERE node_depth = (SELECT MAX(node_depth) FROM prop)
                AND par.id = (SELECT parent_id FROM d WHERE d.id = prop.node_id)
            ) = 1 THEN (
                SELECT node_color FROM prop 
                WHERE node_depth = (SELECT MAX(node_depth) FROM prop)
                AND par.id = (SELECT parent_id FROM d WHERE d.id = prop.node_id)
                LIMIT 1
            )
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(*) FROM prop 
                WHERE node_depth = (SELECT MAX(node_depth) FROM prop)
                AND par.id = (SELECT parent_id FROM d WHERE d.id = prop.node_id)
            ) = 2 THEN (
                SELECT CASE 
                    WHEN MIN(node_color) = MAX(node_color) THEN MIN(node_color)
                    ELSE 'gray'
                END
                FROM prop 
                WHERE node_depth = (SELECT MAX(node_depth) FROM prop)
                AND par.id = (SELECT parent_id FROM d WHERE d.id = prop.node_id)
            )
            ELSE par.color
        END,
        par.depth,
        (SELECT MAX(lvl) FROM prop) + 1
    FROM d par
    WHERE par.color IS NULL
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM prop 
        WHERE node_depth = (SELECT MAX(node_depth) FROM prop)
        AND par.id = (SELECT parent_id FROM d WHERE d.id = prop.node_id)
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        -- Avoid duplicates
        SELECT 1 FROM prop p2 WHERE p2.node_id = par.id
    )
)
-- Update with computed colors
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = (
    SELECT p.node_color 
    FROM prop p 
    WHERE p.node_id = superhero2.id
)
WHERE superhero2.color IS NULL

WITH ordered_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) AS prev_ts,
        -- Detect new group: when previous timestamp is more than 5 minutes ago (or NULL)
        CASE 
            WHEN LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) IS NULL 
                OR JULIANDAY(ts) - JULIANDAY(LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts)) > 5.0/1440.0
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_new_group
    FROM superhero_activities
),
grouped_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) AS group_id
    FROM ordered_activities
)
SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) AS interval_start,
    MAX(ts) AS interval_end,
    -- Calculate total duration in minutes (number of 5-minute intervals * 5)
    (COUNT(*) - 1) * 5 + 5 AS total_minutes,
    COUNT(*) AS num_intervals
FROM grouped_activities
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, group_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, interval_start

WITH RECURSIVE ordered_transactions AS (
    SELECT id, trx, event, result_good,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
    FROM sales_transactions
),
recursive_calc AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT id, trx, event, result_good, rn,
           ROUND((0 + trx) * event, 6) AS result
    FROM ordered_transactions
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: each subsequent row uses previous result
    SELECT ot.id, ot.trx, ot.event, ot.result_good, ot.rn,
           ROUND((rc.result + ot.trx) * ot.event, 6) AS result
    FROM ordered_transactions ot
    JOIN recursive_calc rc ON ot.rn = rc.rn + 1
)
SELECT id, result
FROM recursive_calc
ORDER BY id

cte_with_percent AS (

),
cte_adjusted AS (

    FROM cte_with_percent

FROM cte_adjusted
ORDER BY count DESC, fundingtype

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Parse cards keywords
ck AS (
    SELECT 
        c.id,
        c.keywords AS orig,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(c.keywords, 1, 
            CASE WHEN INSTR(c.keywords, ',') > 0 THEN INSTR(c.keywords, ',') - 1 ELSE LENGTH(c.keywords) END)) AS kw,
        CASE WHEN INSTR(c.keywords, ',') > 0 THEN SUBSTR(c.keywords, INSTR(c.keywords, ',') + 1) ELSE '' END AS rest,
        sq.RemoveKeywords
    FROM cards c
    JOIN SelectQuery sq ON c.id = sq.id
    WHERE c.keywords IS NOT NULL AND c.keywords != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        id, orig,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(rest, 1, 
            CASE WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 THEN INSTR(rest, ',') - 1 ELSE LENGTH(rest) END)),
        CASE WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 THEN SUBSTR(rest, INSTR(rest, ',') + 1) ELSE '' END,
        RemoveKeywords
    FROM ck
    WHERE rest != ''
),
-- Parse remove keywords
rk AS (
    SELECT 
        sq.id,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, 1,
            CASE WHEN INSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, ',') > 0 THEN INSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, ',') - 1 ELSE LENGTH(sq.RemoveKeywords) END)) AS rkw,
        CASE WHEN INSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, ',') > 0 THEN SUBSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, INSTR(sq.RemoveKeywords, ',') + 1) ELSE '' END AS rest
    FROM SelectQuery sq
    WHERE sq.RemoveKeywords IS NOT NULL AND sq.RemoveKeywords != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        id,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(rest, 1,
            CASE WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 THEN INSTR(rest, ',') - 1 ELSE LENGTH(rest) END)),
        CASE WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 THEN SUBSTR(rest, INSTR(rest, ',') + 1) ELSE '' END
    FROM rk
    WHERE rest != ''
),
-- Filtered result
result AS (
    SELECT ck.id, ck.kw
    FROM ck
    WHERE ck.kw != '' AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM rk WHERE rk.id = ck.id AND LOWER(rk.rkw) = LOWER(ck.kw)
    )
)
SELECT 
    c.id,
    COALESCE((SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(r.kw, ', ') FROM result r WHERE r.id = c.id), '') AS updated_keywords
FROM cards c
JOIN SelectQuery sq ON c.id = sq.id
WHERE c.keywords IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY c.id

    circui_name_A1, circui_name_A2, circui_name_A3,
    circui_name_B1, circui_name_B2, circui_name_B3,
    (
        SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(name, ', ') 
        FROM (
            SELECT name 
            FROM (
                SELECT circui_name_A1 AS name UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_A2 UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_A3
            ) 
            WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND name != '' 
            ORDER BY name
        )
    ) AS A_sorted,
    (
        SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(name, ', ') 
        FROM (
            SELECT name 
            FROM (
                SELECT circui_name_B1 AS name UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_B2 UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_B3
            ) 
            WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND name != '' 
            ORDER BY name
        )
    ) AS B_sorted,
    (
        SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(name, '|') 
        FROM (
            SELECT name 
            FROM (
                SELECT circui_name_A1 AS name UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_A2 UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_A3
            ) 
            WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND name != '' 
            ORDER BY name
        )
    ) = (
        SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(name, '|') 
        FROM (
            SELECT name 
            FROM (
                SELECT circui_name_B1 AS name UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_B2 UNION ALL 
                SELECT circui_name_B3
            ) 
            WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND name != '' 
            ORDER BY name
        )
    ) AS are_circuits_same_when_sorted
FROM race_info

WITH daily_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) AS trade_date,
        MIN(date_value) AS first_time,
        datetime(MIN(date_value), '+15 minutes') AS window_end
    FROM card_prices
    GROUP BY card_id, DATE(date_value)
),
prices_with_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        cp.card_id,
        db.trade_date,
        cp.date_value,
        cp.price,
        db.first_time,
        db.window_end,
        CASE WHEN cp.date_value = db.first_time THEN cp.price END AS first_price,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cp.card_id, db.trade_date 
                           ORDER BY cp.date_value DESC) AS rn_desc
    FROM card_prices cp
    JOIN daily_bounds db ON cp.card_id = db.card_id 
        AND DATE(cp.date_value) = db.trade_date
        AND cp.date_value <= db.window_end
),
first_and_last AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        trade_date,
        MAX(first_price) AS first_price,
        MAX(CASE WHEN rn_desc = 1 THEN price END) AS last_price
    FROM prices_with_bounds
    GROUP BY card_id, trade_date
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(last_price - first_price) AS avg_difference
FROM first_and_last
WHERE first_price IS NOT NULL AND last_price IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

    AND SUBSTR(b.bond_id, INSTR(b.bond_id, '_') + INSTR(SUBSTR(b.bond_id, INSTR(b.bond_id, '_') + 1), '_') + 1) 
        = SUBSTR(b2.bond_id, INSTR(b2.bond_id, '_') + 1, INSTR(SUBSTR(b2.bond_id, INSTR(b2.bond_id, '_') + 1), '_') - 1)
    AND b.bond_id != b2.bond_id

SELECT 
    td.years,
    sd.name AS country,
    product_type.name AS product_type,
    lang.name AS language,
    genre.name AS genre,
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN (SELECT vv.* FROM variablevalue vv JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id WHERE v.name = 'ProductType') AS product_type
CROSS JOIN (SELECT vv.* FROM variablevalue vv JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id WHERE v.name = 'publishing_language') AS lang
CROSS JOIN (SELECT vv.* FROM variablevalue vv JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id WHERE v.name = 'genre') AS genre
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dpv_pt
        WHERE dpv_pt.datapoint_id = dp.id AND dpv_pt.variablevalue_id = product_type.id
    )
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dpv_lang
        WHERE dpv_lang.datapoint_id = dp.id AND dpv_lang.variablevalue_id = lang.id
    )
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dpv_genre
        WHERE dpv_genre.datapoint_id = dp.id AND dpv_genre.variablevalue_id = genre.id
    )
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, product_type.name, lang.name, genre.name
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, product_type.name, lang.name, genre.name

WITH RECURSIVE 
events_data AS (
    SELECT match_id, events as event_desc 
    FROM match_events 
    WHERE events IS NOT NULL AND events != ''
),
total AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as total_events FROM events_data
),
tokens AS (
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        1 as position,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(event_desc, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(event_desc, 1, INSTR(event_desc, '#') - 1)
            ELSE event_desc
        END as token,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(event_desc, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(event_desc, INSTR(event_desc, '#') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END as remaining
    FROM events_data
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        position + 1,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(remaining, 1, INSTR(remaining, '#') - 1)
            ELSE remaining
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, '#') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END
    FROM tokens
    WHERE remaining != ''
),
common_tokens AS (
    SELECT position, token
    FROM tokens
    GROUP BY position, token
    HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT total_events FROM total)
),
first_gap AS (
    SELECT MIN(position) as gap_start
    FROM (
        SELECT position FROM common_tokens
        UNION ALL
        SELECT 0
    ) nums
    WHERE position + 1 NOT IN (SELECT position FROM common_tokens)
      AND position >= 0
)
SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(token, '#') 
FROM common_tokens
WHERE position < COALESCE((SELECT gap_start FROM first_gap), 999999)
ORDER BY position

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, extract all key-value pairs from bond_stat_ JSON
bond_types AS (
    SELECT 
        b.bond_id,
        b.molecule_id,
        json_each.key AS type_name,
        json_extract(json_each.value, '$.time') AS time_val,
        json_extract(json_each.value, '$.type') AS type_val
    FROM bond AS b, json_each(b.bond_stat_)
),
-- Calculate total sum of all times per molecule
molecule_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        SUM(CAST(time_val AS REAL)) AS total_time
    FROM bond_types
    GROUP BY molecule_id
),
-- Get bonds with 'Unknown' type and their times
unknown_bonds AS (
    SELECT 
        bt.bond_id,
        bt.molecule_id,
        bt.type_name,
        CAST(bt.time_val AS REAL) AS time_val,
        mt.total_time
    FROM bond_types bt
    JOIN molecule_totals mt ON bt.molecule_id = mt.molecule_id
    WHERE bt.type_val = 'Unknown'
)
SELECT 
    bond_id,
    type_name AS unknown_type,
    time_val AS unknown_time,
    total_time,
    ROUND(time_val / total_time * 100, 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM unknown_bonds
WHERE time_val > 0.1 * total_time
ORDER BY molecule_id, bond_id

-- Trigger to enforce that score must be unique across different (match_id, team_id) combinations
CREATE TRIGGER enforce_unique_score_across_matches
BEFORE INSERT ON match_results
BEGIN
    SELECT CASE
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM match_results
            WHERE score = NEW.score
            AND (match_id != NEW.match_id OR team_id != NEW.team_id)
        )
        THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'Score must be unique across different match and team combinations')
    END;
END;

-- Trigger to enforce the same on UPDATE operations
CREATE TRIGGER enforce_unique_score_across_matches_update
BEFORE UPDATE OF score ON match_results
BEGIN
    SELECT CASE
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM match_results
            WHERE score = NEW.score
            AND (match_id != NEW.match_id OR team_id != NEW.team_id)
            AND rowid != OLD.rowid
        )
        THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'Score must be unique across different match and team combinations')
    END;
END

WITH RECURSIVE date_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        rate,
        DATE(time_span_start) AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN time_span_end IS NULL THEN DATE(time_span_start, '+1 month') 
            ELSE DATE(time_span_end) 
        END AS end_date
    FROM budgets
),
date_series AS (
    SELECT 
        rate,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        start_date AS current_date,
        JULIANDAY(end_date) - JULIANDAY(start_date) AS total_days
    FROM date_ranges
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        rate,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        DATE(current_date, '+1 day'),
        total_days
    FROM date_series
    WHERE current_date < end_date
)
SELECT 
    current_date AS date,
    ROUND(rate / total_days, 2) AS daily_rate
FROM date_series
ORDER BY current_date

WITH RECURSIVE range_values AS (
    SELECT 
        date(MIN(date), 'weekday 0', '-6 days') AS minval, 
        date(MAX(date), 'weekday 0', '-6 days') AS maxval 
    FROM laboratory
), 
week_range AS (
    SELECT minval AS week 
    FROM range_values 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(week, '+7 days') 
    FROM week_range 
    WHERE week < (SELECT maxval FROM range_values)
), 
grouped_labs AS (
    SELECT 
        date(date, 'weekday 0', '-6 days') AS week, 
        COUNT(*) AS total_tests 
    FROM laboratory 
    WHERE date >= '1991-01-01' 
    GROUP BY date(date, 'weekday 0', '-6 days')
) 
SELECT 
    week_range.week, 
    COALESCE(grouped_labs.total_tests, 0) AS total_labs 
FROM week_range 
LEFT OUTER JOIN grouped_labs ON week_range.week = grouped_labs.week 
ORDER BY week_range.week

SELECT 
    m.*,
    ml.latitude,
    ml.longitude,
    (3959 * acos(
        cos(radians(40.730610)) * 
        cos(radians(ml.latitude)) * 
        cos(radians(ml.longitude) - radians(-73.935242)) + 
        sin(radians(40.730610)) * 
        sin(radians(ml.latitude))
    )) AS distance_miles
FROM match m
JOIN match_location ml ON m.id = ml.match_id
WHERE ml.latitude IS NOT NULL 
    AND ml.longitude IS NOT NULL
    AND (3959 * acos(
        cos(radians(40.730610)) * 
        cos(radians(ml.latitude)) * 
        cos(radians(ml.longitude) - radians(-73.935242)) + 
        sin(radians(40.730610)) * 
        sin(radians(ml.latitude))
    )) <= 2
ORDER BY distance_miles

WITH RECURSIVE 
related_curricula(curriculum_id, depth) AS (
  -- Anchor: start with the target curriculum
  SELECT 1, 0
  
  UNION
  
  -- Recursive: find all curricula sharing resources with known curricula
  SELECT DISTINCT cr2.curriculum_id, rc.depth + 1
  FROM related_curricula rc
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr1 ON rc.curriculum_id = cr1.curriculum_id
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 ON cr1.resource_key = cr2.resource_key
  WHERE cr2.curriculum_id != rc.curriculum_id
    AND rc.depth < 10
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM related_curricula)
  AND status = 'installed'

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Generate all dates covered by each event
date_range AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        duration,
        DATE(start_timestamp) as event_date,
        DATE(end_timestamp) as end_date
    FROM event_usage
),
-- Recursive CTE to generate all dates between start and end
all_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        event_date,
        end_date,
        DATETIME(event_date || ' 09:00:00') as work_start,
        DATETIME(event_date || ' 18:00:00') as work_end
    FROM date_range
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        DATE(event_date, '+1 day'),
        end_date,
        DATETIME(DATE(event_date, '+1 day') || ' 09:00:00'),
        DATETIME(DATE(event_date, '+1 day') || ' 18:00:00')
    FROM all_dates
    WHERE event_date < end_date
),
-- Calculate working time for each day
daily_working_time AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        event_date,
        -- Check if weekday (1-5 = Mon-Fri, 0=Sun, 6=Sat)
        CASE 
            WHEN CAST(strftime('%w', event_date) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 5 THEN
                -- Calculate overlap with working hours
                MAX(0, 
                    MIN(
                        CAST((julianday(MIN(end_timestamp, work_end)) - julianday(MAX(start_timestamp, work_start))) * 24 * 60 * 60 AS INTEGER),
                        duration
                    )
                )
            ELSE 0
        END as working_seconds
    FROM all_dates
)
SELECT 
    learner_code, 
    resource_key, 
    SUM(working_seconds) AS total_time_spent 
FROM daily_working_time 
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key

SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    MAX(rating) OVER (
        PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key, grp 
        ORDER BY usage_date
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
    ) AS filled_rating
FROM (
    SELECT 
        *,
        SUM(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
            PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key 
            ORDER BY usage_date 
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) as grp
    FROM resource_usage_2
) sub
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key, usage_date

WITH RECURSIVE date_series AS (
    SELECT DATE('2022-01-01') AS dd 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(dd, '+1 day') 
    FROM date_series 
    WHERE dd < '2022-02-07'
)
SELECT 
    dd AS day_start, 
    COALESCE(SUM(
        CAST(
            MAX(0, 
                (julianday(
                    MIN(datetime(dd, '+1 day'), T.incident_end)
                ) - julianday(
                    MAX(datetime(dd), T.incident_start)
                )) * 1440
            ) 
            AS INTEGER
        )
    ), 0) AS incident_minutes 
FROM date_series 
LEFT JOIN race_incidents AS T 
    ON datetime(T.incident_start) < datetime(dd, '+1 day') 
    AND datetime(T.incident_end) > datetime(dd)
GROUP BY dd 
ORDER BY dd

SELECT 
    p.ID,
    p.SEX,
    p.Birthday,
    p.Diagnosis AS Patient_Diagnosis,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 1 THEN l.Date END) AS Exam_Date_1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 1 THEN l.LDH END) AS LDH_1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 1 THEN l.ALP END) AS ALP_1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 2 THEN l.Date END) AS Exam_Date_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 2 THEN l.LDH END) AS LDH_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 2 THEN l.ALP END) AS ALP_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 3 THEN l.Date END) AS Exam_Date_3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 3 THEN l.LDH END) AS LDH_3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab_rank = 3 THEN l.ALP END) AS ALP_3
FROM Patient p
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        ID,
        Date,
        LDH,
        ALP,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ID ORDER BY Date) AS lab_rank
    FROM Laboratory
) l ON p.ID = l.ID AND l.lab_rank <= 3
GROUP BY p.ID, p.SEX, p.Birthday, p.Diagnosis
ORDER BY p.ID

WITH distinct_dates AS (
    -- First, get distinct dates to avoid counting duplicates multiple times
    SELECT DISTINCT releaseDate 
    FROM sets_releaseInfo
),
numbered AS (
    -- Add row number to each distinct date
    SELECT 
        releaseDate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releaseDate) as rn
    FROM distinct_dates
),
grouped AS (
    -- Create groups: consecutive dates will have the same (releaseDate - rn) value
    SELECT 
        releaseDate,
        DATE(releaseDate, '-' || rn || ' days') as grp
    FROM numbered
),
periods AS (
    -- Group by the calculated group and get min/max dates and count
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) as consecutive_days,
        MIN(releaseDate) as period_start,
        MAX(releaseDate) as period_end
    FROM grouped
    GROUP BY grp
)
-- Filter for periods with more than 1 consecutive day, order by length
SELECT 
    consecutive_days as count,
    period_start as "date MIN",
    period_end as "date MAX"
FROM periods
WHERE consecutive_days > 1
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC, period_start

-- Alternative format with concatenated alphabetical attributes
WITH superhero_performance AS (
    SELECT 
        s.superhero_name,
        DATE('now') AS performance_date,
        a.attribute_name,
        CASE WHEN ha.attribute_value >= 80 THEN 'PASS' ELSE 'FAIL' END AS result
    FROM superhero s
    JOIN hero_attribute ha ON s.id = ha.hero_id
    JOIN attribute a ON ha.attribute_id = a.id
)
SELECT 
    superhero_name,
    performance_date,
    GROUP_CONCAT(attribute_name || ':' || result ORDER BY attribute_name ASC SEPARATOR ', ') AS attribute_results
FROM superhero_performance
GROUP BY superhero_name, performance_date

SELECT 
    (julianday(Date) - julianday(date(strftime('%Y-%m-01', 'now')))) / 7 AS administrative_week,
    COUNT(*) AS record_count
FROM Laboratory 
WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', Date) = strftime('%Y-%m', 'now') 
GROUP BY administrative_week
ORDER BY administrative_week

WITH reporting_dates AS (
    SELECT generate_series AS reporting_date
    FROM generate_series(
        DATE('2000-01-01'),
        DATE('2017-01-01'),
        INTERVAL '1 year'
    )
)
SELECT 
    reporting_date::DATE AS reporting_date,
    COUNT(CASE 
        WHEN OpenDate <= reporting_date 
        AND (ClosedDate IS NULL OR ClosedDate > reporting_date) 
        THEN cdscode 
    END) AS active_schools
FROM reporting_dates
CROSS JOIN schools
GROUP BY reporting_date
ORDER BY reporting_date

WITH filtered_budget AS (
    -- Filter for only Food and Advertisement categories
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        -- Assign priority: Food = 1 (higher priority), Advertisement = 2 (lower priority)
        CASE 
            WHEN category = 'Food' THEN 1
            WHEN category = 'Advertisement' THEN 2
        END AS priority
    FROM budget
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
),
ordered_budget AS (
    -- Order by priority first, then by amount (to maximize number of items, use smaller amounts first)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount) AS item_order
    FROM filtered_budget
),
cumulative_calculation AS (
    -- Calculate cumulative sum across all items (not partitioned by category)
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        priority,
        item_order,
        SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cum_amount
    FROM ordered_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    cum_amount,
    -- Show remaining budget after each purchase
    500 - cum_amount AS remaining_budget,
    -- Indicate if this item can be purchased within the 500 budget
    CASE 
        WHEN cum_amount <= 500 THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS can_purchase
FROM cumulative_calculation
ORDER BY priority, amount

WITH district_1_clients AS (
    SELECT client_id, district_id, 1 as priority
    FROM client
    WHERE district_id = 1
    LIMIT 100
),
other_district_clients AS (
    SELECT client_id, district_id, 2 as priority
    FROM client
    WHERE district_id <> 1
    LIMIT 1000
),
combined AS (
    SELECT * FROM district_1_clients
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM other_district_clients
)
SELECT client_id, district_id
FROM combined
ORDER BY priority
LIMIT 1000

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  SELECT id, nextCardId, CAST(id AS TEXT) AS Path 
  FROM collection 
  WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT nextCardId FROM collection WHERE nextCardId IS NOT NULL) 
  UNION ALL 
  SELECT c.id, c.nextCardId, cte.Path || ' --> ' || CAST(c.id AS TEXT) 
  FROM collection c 
  JOIN path_cte cte ON c.id = cte.nextCardId
) 
SELECT Path 
FROM path_cte 
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL
ORDER BY Path

WITH transaction_data AS (
    SELECT 
        TransactionID as record_id,
        'transaction' as record_type,
        'Amount: ' || Amount || ', Date: ' || Date || ', CustomerID: ' || CustomerID as processed_value,
        Price
    FROM transactions_1k
    WHERE Price > 500
),
customer_data AS (
    SELECT 
        c.CustomerID as record_id,
        'customer' as record_type,
        'Segment: ' || c.Segment || ', Currency: ' || c.Currency as processed_value,
        NULL as Price
    FROM customers c
    INNER JOIN transaction_data t ON c.CustomerID = t.record_id
),
combined_results AS (
    SELECT * FROM transaction_data
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM customer_data
)
SELECT processed_value 
FROM combined_results 
WHERE record_type = 'transaction' AND Price > 500

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Calculate running totals for buys and sells separately
buy_cumulative AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS molecules_before,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS molecules_after
    FROM "transaction"
    WHERE sell = 0
),
sell_cumulative AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS molecules_before,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS molecules_after
    FROM "transaction"
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Generate all possible buy-sell pairs where they overlap
overlaps AS (
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.date AS buy_date,
        s.date AS sell_date,
        b.molecules AS buy_molecules,
        b.usd_total AS buy_usd_total,
        s.molecules AS sell_molecules,
        s.usd_total AS sell_usd_total,
        b.molecules_before AS buy_start,
        b.molecules_after AS buy_end,
        s.molecules_before AS sell_start,
        s.molecules_after AS sell_end,
        -- Calculate overlap
        MAX(b.molecules_before, s.molecules_before) AS overlap_start,
        MIN(b.molecules_after, s.molecules_after) AS overlap_end
    FROM buy_cumulative b
    JOIN sell_cumulative s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    WHERE MAX(b.molecules_before, s.molecules_before) < MIN(b.molecules_after, s.molecules_after)
),
-- Calculate allocated molecules and proportional costs
allocated AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        buy_date,
        sell_date,
        overlap_end - overlap_start AS molecules_allocated,
        buy_usd_total,
        sell_usd_total,
        buy_molecules,
        sell_molecules,
        -- Proportional buy cost: (allocated / total buy molecules) * buy cost
        CAST(overlap_end - overlap_start AS REAL) / buy_molecules * buy_usd_total AS allocated_buy_cost,
        -- Proportional sell value: (allocated / total sell molecules) * sell value
        CAST(overlap_end - overlap_start AS REAL) / sell_molecules * sell_usd_total AS allocated_sell_value
    FROM overlaps
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    molecules_allocated AS molecules,
    ROUND(allocated_buy_cost, 2) AS buy_usd_total,
    ROUND(allocated_sell_value, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM allocated
WHERE molecules_allocated > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

WITH RECURSIVE dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('now') AS date 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(date, '+1 day') 
    FROM dates 
    WHERE date < DATE('now', '+60 days')
), 
schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT sb.*, d.date AS block_date 
    FROM schedule_blocks AS sb 
    JOIN dates AS d 
    ON LOWER(sb.day_of_week) = LOWER(
        CASE CAST(strftime('%w', d.date) AS INTEGER) 
            WHEN 0 THEN 'Sunday' 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Monday' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Tuesday' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Wednesday' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Thursday' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'Friday' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Saturday' 
        END
    ) 
    WHERE sb.is_available = 1 
    AND d.date >= DATE('now') 
    AND d.date <= DATE('now', '+2 months')
) 
SELECT block_id, user_id, block_date AS date, start_time, end_time 
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates 
ORDER BY date, start_time

WITH RECURSIVE ordered_predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prediction_code ASC) as rn
    FROM performance_predictions
),
A(GroupNum, RollingSum, prediction_code, Performance_Prediction, rn) AS (
    SELECT 
        1, 
        Performance_Prediction, 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        rn
    FROM ordered_predictions 
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN A.GroupNum + 1 
            ELSE A.GroupNum 
        END, 
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN P.Performance_Prediction 
            ELSE A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction 
        END,
        P.prediction_code,
        P.Performance_Prediction,
        P.rn
    FROM A 
    JOIN ordered_predictions AS P ON A.rn + 1 = P.rn
)
SELECT 
    GroupNum, 
    SUM(Performance_Prediction) AS group_sum
FROM A 
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

SELECT DISTINCT A.client_id AS child 
FROM client AS A, client AS B 
WHERE A.district_id = B.district_id 
  AND A.client_id != B.client_id 
  AND julianday(B.birth_date) + (16 * 365.25) < julianday(A.birth_date)
ORDER BY A.client_id

SELECT DISTINCT c1.client_id
FROM client c1
JOIN client c2 ON c1.district_id = c2.district_id 
    AND c1.client_id != c2.client_id
WHERE (julianday(c2.birth_date) - julianday(c1.birth_date)) / 365.25 >= 16
ORDER BY c1.client_id

WITH member_event_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) AS attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
member_ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_event) AS member_rank
    FROM member_event_counts
),
event_ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_member) AS event_rank
    FROM member_event_counts
)
SELECT link_to_member, link_to_event, attendance_count AS total
FROM member_ranked
WHERE member_rank <= 2

UNION

SELECT link_to_member, link_to_event, attendance_count AS total
FROM event_ranked
WHERE event_rank = 1

ORDER BY link_to_member, link_to_event

-- Top 2 gas stations (events) per customer based on transaction count
-- AND Top 1 customer per gas station (event) based on transaction count

WITH customer_station_counts AS (
    -- Count transactions per customer per gas station
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        GasStationID,
        COUNT(*) as attendance_count
    FROM transactions_1k
    GROUP BY CustomerID, GasStationID
),
ranked_by_customer AS (
    -- Rank gas stations for each customer by attendance count
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        GasStationID,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, GasStationID) as customer_rank
    FROM customer_station_counts
),
ranked_by_station AS (
    -- Rank customers for each gas station by attendance count
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        GasStationID,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY GasStationID ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, CustomerID) as station_rank
    FROM customer_station_counts
)
-- Get top 2 gas stations per customer
SELECT 'Top 2 Stations per Customer' as category, CustomerID, GasStationID, attendance_count, customer_rank as rank
FROM ranked_by_customer
WHERE customer_rank <= 2

UNION ALL

-- Get top 1 customer per gas station
SELECT 'Top 1 Customer per Station' as category, CustomerID, GasStationID, attendance_count, station_rank as rank
FROM ranked_by_station
WHERE station_rank = 1

ORDER BY category, CustomerID, rank

WITH RECURSIVE split_engagement AS (
    -- Base case: select the learner_code and the first engagement value
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        engagement_history,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(engagement_history, 1, 
            CASE 
                WHEN INSTR(engagement_history, ',') > 0 
                THEN INSTR(engagement_history, ',') - 1 
                ELSE LENGTH(engagement_history) 
            END)) AS engagement,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(engagement_history, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(engagement_history, INSTR(engagement_history, ',') + 1)
            ELSE '' 
        END AS remaining
    FROM learners_array
    WHERE engagement_history IS NOT NULL AND engagement_history != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: process the remaining string
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        engagement_history,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, 1, 
            CASE 
                WHEN INSTR(remaining, ',') > 0 
                THEN INSTR(remaining, ',') - 1 
                ELSE LENGTH(remaining) 
            END)) AS engagement,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ',') + 1)
            ELSE '' 
        END AS remaining
    FROM split_engagement
    WHERE remaining != ''
)
SELECT 
    learner_code,
    CAST(engagement AS INTEGER) AS engagement
FROM split_engagement
WHERE engagement IS NOT NULL AND engagement != ''
ORDER BY learner_code, engagement

SELECT 
    l.learner_code, 
    CASE 
        WHEN l.Geographic_Location = 'Urban' THEN 1 
        WHEN l.Geographic_Location = 'Rural' THEN 2 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS location_id, 
    AVG(o.Pre_Test_Score) AS average_pre_test_score 
FROM learners l
LEFT JOIN outcomes o ON l.learner_code = o.learner_code
WHERE l.Engagement_History > 5 
    AND l.Gender = 'Female' 
    AND l.Language_Proficiency IN ('Basic', 'Intermediate') 
    AND (
        (${socio_status} IN ('Low', 'Middle') AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Urban')
        OR 
        (${socio_status} = 'High' AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Rural')
    )
GROUP BY l.learner_code, l.Geographic_Location

WITH src AS (
    SELECT 1 as id, 0.0 as x1, 0.0 as y1, 0.0 as x2, 10.0 as y2 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 2, 0.0, 10.0, 0.0, 11.0 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 3, 1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 5.0 UNION ALL 
    SELECT 4, 1.0, 1.0, 2.0, 2.0
),
-- Calculate intersection using cross product method
intersections AS (
    SELECT 
        a.id as id_a,
        b.id as id_b,
        -- Calculate the denominator for line intersection
        (a.x1 - a.x2) * (b.y1 - b.y2) - (a.y1 - a.y2) * (b.x1 - b.x2) as denom,
        -- Calculate intersection point parameters
        ((a.x1 - b.x1) * (b.y1 - b.y2) - (a.y1 - b.y1) * (b.x1 - b.x2)) as t_num,
        ((a.x1 - b.x1) * (a.y1 - a.y2) - (a.y1 - b.y1) * (a.x1 - a.x2)) as u_num
    FROM src AS a
    JOIN src AS b ON a.id < b.id
),
-- Find actual intersections that are interior to both segments
interior_crossings AS (
    SELECT 
        id_a, 
        id_b,
        CASE 
            WHEN denom = 0 THEN 0 -- Parallel or collinear
            WHEN CAST(t_num AS REAL) / denom > 0.001 
                 AND CAST(t_num AS REAL) / denom < 0.999
                 AND CAST(u_num AS REAL) / denom > 0.001
                 AND CAST(u_num AS REAL) / denom < 0.999 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END as is_interior_crossing
    FROM intersections
    WHERE denom != 0
)
SELECT DISTINCT id_a as id FROM interior_crossings WHERE is_interior_crossing = 1
UNION
SELECT DISTINCT id_b as id FROM interior_crossings WHERE is_interior_crossing = 1

WITH params AS (
    SELECT 
        '2023-01-01 10:34:36' as start_ts,
        '2023-01-10 15:12:24' as end_ts
),
parsed AS (
    SELECT 
        datetime(start_ts) as start_dt,
        datetime(end_ts) as end_dt,
        CAST(strftime('%s', start_ts) AS INTEGER) as start_epoch,
        CAST(strftime('%s', end_ts) AS INTEGER) as end_epoch
    FROM params
),
date_series AS (
    SELECT date(start_dt, '+' || n || ' days') as dt
    FROM parsed
    JOIN (SELECT 0 as n UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 
          UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7
          UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10 UNION ALL SELECT 11
          UNION ALL SELECT 12 UNION ALL SELECT 13 UNION ALL SELECT 14) as nums
    WHERE dt <= date(end_dt)
),
weekend_days AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt 
    FROM date_series 
    WHERE CAST(strftime('%w', dt) AS INTEGER) IN (0, 6)
),
business_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        (end_epoch - start_epoch - cnt * 86400) as total_secs
    FROM parsed, weekend_days
),
components AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(total_secs / 86400 AS INTEGER) as days,
        CAST((total_secs % 86400) / 3600 AS INTEGER) as hours,
        CAST((total_secs % 3600) / 60 AS INTEGER) as minutes,
        CAST(total_secs % 60 AS INTEGER) as seconds
    FROM business_seconds
)
SELECT 
    days || ' days ' || 
    hours || ' hours ' || 
    minutes || ' minutes ' || 
    seconds || ' seconds' as time_diff
FROM components

WITH links AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN link.source_cluster > link.target_cluster THEN link.source_cluster ELSE link.target_cluster END AS big, 
        CASE WHEN link.source_cluster > link.target_cluster THEN link.target_cluster ELSE link.source_cluster END AS smol, 
        link.type AS type 
    FROM link
)
SELECT 
    l.cluster AS left_id, 
    l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
    l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
    r.cluster AS right_id, 
    r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
    r.cluster_label AS right_label, 
    COUNT(DISTINCT l.article) AS articles, 
    GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT links.type) AS link_types 
FROM tag AS l 
JOIN tag AS r ON l.article = r.article AND l.cluster > r.cluster 
LEFT JOIN links ON l.cluster = links.big AND r.cluster = links.smol 
GROUP BY l.cluster, l.cluster_label, l.cluster_type, r.cluster, r.cluster_label, r.cluster_type 
ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT l.article) DESC

         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY o.established DESC) as rn
  FROM ismember AS i 
  INNER JOIN organization AS o ON o.abbreviation = i.organization 
  WHERE i.type = 'member' AND i.country = 'CI'
) sub
WHERE rn = 1

SELECT 
    AVG(CASE WHEN inst."Institution_Type" = 'Public' THEN outcm."Pre_Test_Score" END) AS public_avg_score,
    AVG(CASE WHEN inst."Institution_Type" = 'Private' THEN outcm."Pre_Test_Score" END) AS private_avg_score,
    AVG(CASE WHEN inst."Institution_Type" = 'Public' THEN outcm."Pre_Test_Score" END) - 
    AVG(CASE WHEN inst."Institution_Type" = 'Private' THEN outcm."Pre_Test_Score" END) AS score_diff
FROM outcomes AS outcm
JOIN enrollment AS enrl ON outcm.learner_code = enrl.learner_code
JOIN institutions AS inst ON enrl.institution_key = inst.institution_key
WHERE inst."Institution_Type" IN ('Public', 'Private')

SELECT REPLACE(REPLACE(COALESCE(primary_pathology_histological_type, ''), 'NOS', 'Not Otherwise Specified'), 'Adenocarcinoma', 'Adenoma Carcinoma') AS transformed_histological_type FROM pathology_and_surgery

        art,
        apop,
        rdt,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY art ORDER BY rdt DESC) as rn
    FROM sp_album_ctb c
    JOIN sp_albums a ON c.alb = a.zid
    WHERE a.rdt IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    art,
    SUM(apop) as total_popularity

WITH pathology_with_condition AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        CASE 
            WHEN COALESCE(primary_pathology_initial_pathologic_diagnosis_method, 
                         primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) 
                 LIKE 'Other method, specify:' 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS should_redact
    FROM pathology_and_surgery
)
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN should_redact = 1 THEN NULL 
         ELSE primary_pathology_neoplasm_histologic_grade 
    END AS histologic_grade,
    CASE WHEN should_redact = 1 THEN NULL 
         ELSE primary_pathology_days_to_initial_pathologic_diagnosis 
    END AS days_to_initial_diagnosis,
    CASE WHEN should_redact = 1 THEN NULL 
         ELSE primary_pathology_age_at_initial_pathologic_diagnosis 
    END AS age_at_initial_diagnosis
FROM pathology_with_condition

WITH with_prev AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        LAG(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS prev_end
    FROM sp_album_ctb
),
with_group AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        SUM(CASE WHEN DATE(start_date, '-1 day') = prev_end THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS grp
    FROM with_prev
)
SELECT 
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    MIN(start_date) AS start_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN end_date IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN NULL
        ELSE MAX(end_date) 
    END AS end_date
FROM with_group
GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
ORDER BY alb, art, start_date

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Split the delivery_zones by comma
split_parts(order_id, customer_id, product_id, delivery_zones, part, remaining) AS (
    SELECT 
        order_id, 
        customer_id, 
        product_id, 
        delivery_zones,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(delivery_zones, 1, 
            CASE WHEN INSTR(delivery_zones, ',') = 0 
                 THEN LENGTH(delivery_zones) 
                 ELSE INSTR(delivery_zones, ',') - 1 
            END)),
        CASE WHEN INSTR(delivery_zones, ',') = 0 
             THEN '' 
             ELSE SUBSTR(delivery_zones, INSTR(delivery_zones, ',') + 1) 
        END
    FROM orders
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        order_id, 
        customer_id, 
        product_id, 
        delivery_zones,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, 1, 
            CASE WHEN INSTR(remaining, ',') = 0 
                 THEN LENGTH(remaining) 
                 ELSE INSTR(remaining, ',') - 1 
            END)),
        CASE WHEN INSTR(remaining, ',') = 0 
             THEN '' 
             ELSE SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ',') + 1) 
        END
    FROM split_parts
    WHERE remaining != ''
),
-- Parse each part to get start and end of range
parsed_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        order_id, 
        customer_id, 
        product_id, 
        delivery_zones,
        part,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(part, '-') > 0 
            THEN CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(part, 1, INSTR(part, '-') - 1)) AS INTEGER)
            ELSE CAST(part AS INTEGER)
        END as range_start,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(part, '-') > 0 
            THEN CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(part, INSTR(part, '-') + 1)) AS INTEGER)
            ELSE CAST(part AS INTEGER)
        END as range_end
    FROM split_parts
    WHERE part != ''
)
SELECT DISTINCT order_id, customer_id, product_id, delivery_zones
FROM parsed_ranges
WHERE 10015 >= range_start AND 10015 <= range_end
ORDER BY order_id

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start with all sets that have a parent
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        parentCode,
        code AS path
    FROM sets
    WHERE parentCode IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with parent sets to walk up the tree
    SELECT 
        h.id,
        h.code,
        s.parentCode,
        s.code || '-' || h.path
    FROM hierarchy h
    JOIN sets s ON h.parentCode = s.code
    WHERE s.parentCode IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    h1.id,
    h1.code,
    h1.path AS ancestralCode
FROM hierarchy h1
LEFT JOIN hierarchy h2 ON h1.id = h2.id AND h1.path != h2.path AND h2.path LIKE h1.path || '%'
WHERE h2.id IS NULL
ORDER BY h1.id

SELECT 
    link_to_event,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Closed'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
             AND SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS consolidated_status,
    CASE 
        -- If any 'Closed', get max remaining from 'Closed' entries
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining END)
        -- If both 'Open' and 'Planning' exist (but no 'Closed'), sum of max spent from each
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
             AND SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
            THEN (MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END) 
                  + MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END))
        -- If only 'Open', max spent from 'Open'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END)
        -- If only 'Planning', max spent from 'Planning'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS financial_value
FROM budget 
GROUP BY link_to_event

WITH RECURSIVE 
TreeData AS (
    SELECT 
        entityid, 
        parentid, 
        emission, 
        COALESCE(pctcontribution, 100) AS pctcontribution
    FROM tree_table
),
-- Calculate depth of each node (distance from root)
NodeDepth AS (
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        pctcontribution,
        0 AS depth
    FROM TreeData
    WHERE parentid IS NULL OR parentid NOT IN (SELECT entityid FROM TreeData)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        t.pctcontribution,
        nd.depth + 1
    FROM TreeData AS t
    JOIN NodeDepth AS nd ON t.parentid = nd.entityid
),
-- Bottom-up: start from deepest nodes
CumulativeEmission AS (
    -- Get max depth first
    SELECT 
        nd.entityid,
        nd.parentid,
        nd.emission AS own_emission,
        CAST(nd.emission AS REAL) AS cumulative_emission,
        nd.pctcontribution,
        nd.depth,
        (SELECT MAX(depth) FROM NodeDepth) AS current_calc_depth
    FROM NodeDepth AS nd
    WHERE nd.depth = (SELECT MAX(depth) FROM NodeDepth)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Move up one level at a time
    SELECT 
        nd.entityid,
        nd.parentid,
        nd.emission AS own_emission,
        CAST(
            nd.emission + 
            COALESCE((
                SELECT SUM(ce.cumulative_emission * ce.pctcontribution / 100.0)
                FROM CumulativeEmission AS ce
                WHERE ce.parentid = nd.entityid
                  AND ce.current_calc_depth = CumulativeEmission.current_calc_depth
            ), 0)
            AS REAL
        ) AS cumulative_emission,
        nd.pctcontribution,
        nd.depth,
        CumulativeEmission.current_calc_depth - 1
    FROM NodeDepth AS nd
    WHERE nd.depth = CumulativeEmission.current_calc_depth - 1
)
SELECT 
    entityid, 
    parentid, 
    cumulative_emission AS total_emission
FROM CumulativeEmission
ORDER BY entityid

CREATE TRIGGER profile_trigger 
AFTER INSERT ON users 
FOR EACH ROW 
BEGIN 
    INSERT INTO profiles (id, CreationDate, WebsiteUrl) 
    VALUES (
        NEW.Id, 
        NEW.CreationDate, 
        CASE 
            WHEN NEW.WebsiteUrl IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN INSTR(LOWER(NEW.WebsiteUrl), 'http://') = 1 THEN 
                SUBSTR(NEW.WebsiteUrl, 8, INSTR(LOWER(NEW.WebsiteUrl), '.com') - 8)
            WHEN INSTR(LOWER(NEW.WebsiteUrl), 'https://') = 1 THEN 
                SUBSTR(NEW.WebsiteUrl, 9, INSTR(LOWER(NEW.WebsiteUrl), '.com') - 9)
            ELSE 
                SUBSTR(NEW.WebsiteUrl, 1, INSTR(LOWER(NEW.WebsiteUrl), '.com') - 1)
        END
    );
END

WITH RECURSIVE
all_points AS (
    SELECT start AS ts FROM duration WHERE start IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT "end" AS ts FROM duration WHERE "end" IS NOT NULL
),
sorted_points AS (
    SELECT ts, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ts) AS rn
    FROM all_points
),
intervals AS (
    SELECT 
        s1.ts AS interval_start,
        s2.ts AS interval_end
    FROM sorted_points s1
    JOIN sorted_points s2 ON s1.rn = s2.rn - 1
)
SELECT 
    i.interval_start,
    i.interval_end,
    COUNT(d.id) AS concurrent_events
FROM intervals i
JOIN duration d ON d.start < i.interval_end AND d."end" > i.interval_start
GROUP BY i.interval_start, i.interval_end
ORDER BY concurrent_events DESC, i.interval_start

WITH RECURSIVE atom_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start from 'TR007_8'
    SELECT 
        atom_id, 
        atom_id2, 
        bond_id, 
        1 AS level, 
        atom_id || ',' || atom_id2 AS path
    FROM connected 
    WHERE atom_id = 'TR007_8'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find next connections
    SELECT 
        c.atom_id, 
        c.atom_id2, 
        c.bond_id, 
        ah.level + 1, 
        ah.path || ',' || c.atom_id2
    FROM connected AS c 
    JOIN atom_hierarchy AS ah ON c.atom_id = ah.atom_id2
    -- Prevent cycles: check if the next node (atom_id2) is already in the path
    WHERE INSTR(ah.path, ',' || c.atom_id2 || ',') = 0
      AND ah.path NOT LIKE '%' || c.atom_id2
)
SELECT 
    atom_id, 
    atom_id2, 
    bond_id, 
    level 
FROM atom_hierarchy 
ORDER BY level, atom_id

),
"GroupSums" AS (

WITH target_pairs(mol_id1, mol_id2) AS (
    VALUES ('TR427', 'TR430'), ('TR427', 'TR431'), ('TR430', 'TR431')
),
molecule_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        m.molecule_id,
        m.label,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.atom_id) AS atom_count,
        COUNT(DISTINCT b.bond_id) AS bond_count,
        GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a.element) AS elements
    FROM molecule AS m
    LEFT JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    LEFT JOIN bond AS b ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
    GROUP BY m.molecule_id, m.label
)
SELECT 
    m1.molecule_id AS molecule_id1,
    m1.label AS label1,
    m1.atom_count AS atom_count1,
    m1.elements AS elements1,
    m2.molecule_id AS molecule_id2,
    m2.label AS label2,
    m2.atom_count AS atom_count2,
    m2.elements AS elements2,
    CASE 
        WHEN m1.bond_count > 0 AND m2.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Both have bonds'
        WHEN m1.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Only ' || m1.molecule_id || ' has bonds'
        WHEN m2.bond_count > 0 THEN 'Only ' || m2.molecule_id || ' has bonds'
        ELSE 'Neither has bonds'
    END AS bond_status
FROM target_pairs AS tp
JOIN molecule_stats AS m1 ON m1.molecule_id = tp.mol_id1
JOIN molecule_stats AS m2 ON m2.molecule_id = tp.mol_id2
ORDER BY tp.mol_id1, tp.mol_id2
SELECT "m"."molecule_id", "m"."label", "a"."element" FROM molecule AS "m" INNER JOIN atom AS "a" ON "m"."molecule_id" = "a"."molecule_id" ORDER BY "m"."molecule_id", "a"."atom_id"

SELECT

        WHEN a.element = 'c' THEN 'Carbon'
        WHEN a.element = 'h' THEN 'Hydrogen'
        WHEN a.element = 'o' THEN 'Oxygen'
        WHEN a.element = 'n' THEN 'Nitrogen'
        ELSE a.element
    END AS element_name,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.molecule_id ORDER BY a.atom_id) AS atom_rank,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.molecule_id ORDER BY b.bond_type) AS bond_type_rank
FROM molecule AS m
LEFT OUTER JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
LEFT OUTER JOIN connected AS c ON a.atom_id = c.atom_id
LEFT OUTER JOIN bond AS b ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, a.atom_id, b.bond_id

WITH type_years AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        CAST(strftime('%Y', event_date) AS INTEGER) AS year
    FROM event
    WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL
),
year_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        year,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM type_years
    GROUP BY type, year
),
type_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        type,
        MIN(year) AS earliest_year,
        MAX(year) AS latest_year
    FROM type_years
    GROUP BY type
),
earliest_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        tb.type,
        yc.cnt AS earliest_count
    FROM type_bounds tb
    JOIN year_counts yc ON tb.type = yc.type AND tb.earliest_year = yc.year
),
latest_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        tb.type,
        yc.cnt AS latest_count
    FROM type_bounds tb
    JOIN year_counts yc ON tb.type = yc.type AND tb.latest_year = yc.year
)
SELECT 
    tb.type AS event_type,
    lc.latest_count - ec.earliest_count AS year_difference
FROM type_bounds tb
JOIN earliest_counts ec ON tb.type = ec.type
JOIN latest_counts lc ON tb.type = lc.type
ORDER BY tb.type

    FROM atom AS a
), 
temp2 AS (

     )
SELECT

    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.id IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_not_identical_in_b

LEFT JOIN set_b AS b ON all_ids.id = b.id

SELECT 
    match_id,
    match_date,
    CAST(ROUND(
        CASE 
            WHEN CAST(strftime('%w', match_date) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 5 THEN
                MAX(0.0, 
                    (julianday(effective_end) - julianday(effective_start)) * 1440.0
                )
            ELSE 0 
        END
    ) AS INTEGER) AS match_duration_minutes
FROM (
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        match_date,
        MAX(
            datetime(date(match_date) || ' 07:30:00'),
            match_date
        ) AS effective_start,
        MIN(
            datetime(date(match_date) || ' 17:00:00'),
            datetime(match_date, '+2 hours')
        ) AS effective_end
    FROM detail
)

WITH tiers AS (
    SELECT 1 AS from_hour, 4 AS to_hour, 20 AS pay 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5 AS from_hour, 8 AS to_hour, 50 AS pay 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 10 AS from_hour, 12 AS to_hour, 100 AS pay
), 
input_data AS (
    SELECT 6 AS user_hours
) 
SELECT 
    i.user_hours, 
    SUM(
        t.pay * MAX(0, MIN(i.user_hours, t.to_hour) - t.from_hour + 1)
    ) AS sum_to_pay 
FROM input_data AS i 
CROSS JOIN tiers AS t 
WHERE t.from_hour <= i.user_hours

WITH home_wins AS (
    -- Identify home wins based on match_details JSON
    -- This assumes we can determine winner from the JSON structure
    SELECT 
        home_team AS team,
        match_date,
        -- A home win when home team players scored more than away team players
        CASE 
            WHEN (
                SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CAST(json_extract(value, '$.goals') AS INTEGER)), 0)
                FROM json_each(match_details, '$.player_stats')
                WHERE json_extract(value, '$.team') = home_team
            ) > (
                SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CAST(json_extract(value, '$.goals') AS INTEGER)), 0)
                FROM json_each(match_details, '$.player_stats')
                WHERE json_extract(value, '$.team') != home_team
            ) THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_win
    FROM detail
),
numbered AS (
    SELECT 
        team,
        match_date,
        is_win,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team ORDER BY match_date) AS rn,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team, is_win ORDER BY match_date) AS rn_is_win
    FROM home_wins
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        team,
        match_date,
        rn - rn_is_win AS streak_id
    FROM numbered
    WHERE is_win = 1
)
SELECT 
    team,
    MIN(match_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(match_date) AS end_date,
    COUNT(*) AS match_count
FROM streak_groups
GROUP BY team, streak_id
ORDER BY team, start_date

WITH ranked_atoms AS (
    SELECT 
        atom_id,
        molecule_id,
        element,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY atom_id) as rn
    FROM atom
)
SELECT atom_id, molecule_id, element
FROM ranked_atoms
WHERE rn <= 2
ORDER BY molecule_id, atom_id

WITH RECURSIVE split_dates AS (
    -- Base case
    SELECT 
        id,
        date AS remaining,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(date, ' ') = 0 THEN date
            ELSE TRIM(SUBSTR(date, 1, INSTR(date, ' 00:00:00') + 9))
        END AS date_part
    FROM Player_Attributes
    WHERE date IS NOT NULL AND date != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case
    SELECT 
        id,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1)) AS remaining,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1)), ' ') = 0 
                THEN TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1))
            ELSE TRIM(SUBSTR(TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1)), 1, 
                INSTR(TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1)), ' 00:00:00') + 9))
        END AS date_part
    FROM split_dates
    WHERE TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, LENGTH(date_part) + 1)) != ''
)
SELECT id, date_part 
FROM split_dates 
WHERE date_part != '' AND date_part IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY id, date_part

    SELECT DATE('2018-01-01') AS month_start_date, 1 AS month_num 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(month_start_date, '+1 month'), month_num + 1 
    FROM month_generator 
    WHERE month_num < 12
) 
SELECT mg.month_start_date, m.molecule_id, m.label 
FROM month_generator AS mg 
CROSS JOIN molecule AS m 
ORDER BY mg.month_start_date, m.molecule_id

WITH tbl AS (
    SELECT date(substr('12.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('12.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('12.01.2023', 1, 2)) AS act_date, 'mol1' AS mol_id FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('19.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol2' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('19.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol3' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('19.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol4' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('19.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('19.01.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol5' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('12.02.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('12.02.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('12.02.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol6' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('31.01.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('31.01.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('31.01.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol7' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('13.02.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('13.02.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('13.02.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol8' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('17.02.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('17.02.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('17.02.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol9' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('17.02.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('17.02.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('17.02.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol10' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('19.02.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('19.02.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('19.02.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol11' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('10.03.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('10.03.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('10.03.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol12' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('12.03.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('12.03.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('12.03.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol13' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('27.03.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('27.03.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('27.03.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol14' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy UNION ALL 
    SELECT date(substr('10.04.2023', 7, 4) || '-' || substr('10.04.2023', 4, 2) || '-' || substr('10.04.2023', 1, 2)), 'mol15' FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy
),
months AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        STRFTIME('MON-%y', act_date) AS month_label,
        STRFTIME('%Y%m', act_date) AS month_sort
    FROM tbl
),
grid AS (
    SELECT 
        m1.month_label AS activation_month,
        m1.month_sort AS rn_1,
        m2.month_label AS act_month,
        m2.month_sort AS rn_2
    FROM months m1
    JOIN months m2 ON m1.month_sort <= m2.month_sort
),
molecule_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        mol_id,
        STRFTIME('%Y%m', act_date) AS act_month
    FROM tbl
)
SELECT 
    g.activation_month,
    g.act_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ma.mol_id) AS active_molecules
FROM grid g
LEFT JOIN molecule_activity ma 
    ON ma.act_month BETWEEN g.rn_1 AND g.rn_2
GROUP BY 
    g.activation_month,
    g.act_month,
    g.rn_1,
    g.rn_2
ORDER BY 
    g.rn_1, 
    g.rn_2

WITH "credits" AS (
    SELECT 5001 AS "ID", 12 AS "ARTCRED", 7.5 AS "BIZCRED", 12 AS "HUMCRED", 14 AS "NATCRED", 11 AS "TEKCRED", 9 AS "GENCRED" 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5002 AS "ID", 10.5 AS "ARTCRED", 6 AS "BIZCRED", 5 AS "HUMCRED", 4 AS "NATCRED", 6 AS "TEKCRED", 3 AS "GENCRED"
), 
"grp_choices" AS (
    SELECT 5001 AS "ID", 'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE" 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5001 AS "ID", 'BIZ' AS "GROUPCHOICE" 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5001 AS "ID", 'NAT' AS "GROUPCHOICE" 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 5002 AS "ID", 'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE"
) 
SELECT 
    G."ID", 
    G."GROUPCHOICE", 
    CASE 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'ART' THEN C."ARTCRED" 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'BIZ' THEN C."BIZCRED" 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'HUM' THEN C."HUMCRED" 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'NAT' THEN C."NATCRED" 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'TEK' THEN C."TEKCRED" 
        WHEN G."GROUPCHOICE" = 'GEN' THEN C."GENCRED" 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS "CREDIT" 
FROM "grp_choices" AS G
JOIN "credits" AS C ON G."ID" = C."ID"
ORDER BY G."ID", G."GROUPCHOICE"

    WHERE TransactionID IN (
        SELECT TransactionID 
        FROM transactions_1k 
        WHERE Amount = 50 
        ORDER BY Date 
        LIMIT 100
    )
    RETURNING TransactionID, CustomerID
)
SELECT u.TransactionID, c.Segment
FROM updated u
JOIN customers c ON u.CustomerID = c.CustomerID

WITH player_data AS (
    SELECT 
        p.player_api_id AS player_id, 
        'name' AS attribute_type, 
        p.player_name AS attribute_value 
    FROM player AS p 
    WHERE p.player_api_id IS NOT NULL 
    
    UNION ALL 
    
    SELECT 
        pa.player_api_id AS player_id, 
        'rating' AS attribute_type, 
        CAST(pa.overall_rating AS TEXT) AS attribute_value 
    FROM player_attributes AS pa 
    WHERE pa.date = (
        SELECT MAX(date) 
        FROM player_attributes 
        WHERE player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    ) 
    
    UNION ALL 
    
    SELECT 
        pa.player_api_id AS player_id, 
        'foot' AS attribute_type, 
        pa.preferred_foot AS attribute_value 
    FROM player_attributes AS pa 
    WHERE pa.date = (
        SELECT MAX(date) 
        FROM player_attributes 
        WHERE player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    )
)
SELECT 
    player_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN attribute_type = 'name' THEN attribute_value END) AS name,
    MAX(CASE WHEN attribute_type = 'rating' THEN attribute_value END) AS rating,
    MAX(CASE WHEN attribute_type = 'foot' THEN attribute_value END) AS preferred_foot
FROM player_data
GROUP BY player_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT attribute_type) = 3
ORDER BY CAST(COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN attribute_type = 'rating' THEN attribute_value END), '0') AS REAL) DESC
LIMIT 10

    SELECT '1111' AS TECHNICAL_ID, 'NOTIONALCR' AS ASSET_TYPE 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT '1111' AS TECHNICAL_ID, '50000' AS ASSET_TYPE 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT '2222' AS TECHNICAL_ID, 'FWDNOTLCR' AS ASSET_TYPE 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT '2222' AS TECHNICAL_ID, '50000' AS ASSET_TYPE 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT '3333' AS TECHNICAL_ID, '50000' AS ASSET_TYPE 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT '3333' AS TECHNICAL_ID, 'DUMMY' AS ASSET_TYPE
), 
MAP_RECRF_ASSET_TYPE AS (
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'NOTIONALCR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 1 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'NOTIONALDB' AS ASSET_TYPE, 1 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'FWDNOTLCR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'FWDNOTLDR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'SWOFFBALCR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'SWOFFBALDR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'SWFWNOTLCR' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, 'SWFWNOTLDB' AS ASSET_TYPE, 2 AS PRIORITY 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'SW' AS APPLICATION, '50000' AS ASSET_TYPE, 3 AS PRIORITY
), 
CTE AS (
    SELECT 
        X.TECHNICAL_ID, 
        X.ASSET_TYPE, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY X.TECHNICAL_ID ORDER BY MRAT.PRIORITY ASC) AS rn 
    FROM MY_TABLE AS X
